title: achillea erba-rotta subsp. rupestris
intro: Achillea erba-rotta subsp. rupestris, also known as the Rock Yarrow or Mountain Yarrow, is a subspecies of the Yarrow plant (Achillea) native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This perennial herb has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Achillea erba-rotta subsp. rupestris include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, tinctures, or dried flowers for use in traditional medicine, and its leaves and flowers can also be used in cooking and as a natural dye.
